sprint: My Projects -- Reach an Existing Project Without Going Through Creation Flow
task: A real, user-requested gap, confirmed via investigation: Creator's own persistence has always been a single fixed localStorage slot -- one active project, no way to see or reopen anything else without wiping it. Confirmed with the user directly that a genuine multi-project store was wanted, mirroring World Builder's own proven ProjectStore pattern. New js/creatorProjectStore.js is purely additive bookkeeping riding alongside the existing single-session slot, never replacing it. js/projectManager.js gained a small hook: every project gets a stable id the first time it's ever autosaved, and every successful autosave also upserts the current project into the new store. js/creationFlow.js's Screen 1 gained a small 'My Projects' pill (shown only once at least one project exists) sitting above the six Creation Type cards -- reachable before any Creation Type is chosen -- opening a small list screen (thumbnail/name/edited-time, newest first); tapping a project opens it directly, bypassing Screen 1/2 entirely. Verified via Playwright using real page reloads between two separately-authored projects: two distinct records with distinct ids, correct ordering and names, correct reopen behaviour preserving the original id, and the pre-existing single-session restore-modal flow confirmed completely unaffected. Full regression across the existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors.
